title: The tactile triangle: a design research framework demonstrated through tactile comparisons of textile materials
summary: This paper proposes the tactile triangle as a three-level framework for understanding touch through physical properties, perceptual experience, and communication. It treats touch as something measurable, perceivable, and communicable rather than only as a sensory event. It is useful as a transferable model for analysing touch across domains beyond textiles.

related.reason: Both expand touch beyond narrow technical framing.
Both emphasize bodily and perceptual meaning beyond neutral input.
Both relate to tactile evaluation and textile material perception.

notes: This is one of the most useful conceptual papers in the set because it gives a structure for talking about touch at multiple levels. The model can be mapped onto interface design through physical, perceptual, and communicative layers.
quotes: Touch is positioned simultaneously as measurable, perceivable, and communicable.
